audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for temple prediction, Olivet discourse, birth-pains, persecution, abomination/desolation, false messiahs, cosmic signs, Son of Humanity, fig tree, unknown day/hour, and watchfulness.

verses.restored: As he was going out from the temple, one of his disciples said to him, Teacher, look, what stones and what buildings!
Jesus said to him, Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here on another that will not be thrown down.
As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew questioned him privately,
Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be completed?
Jesus began to say to them, See that no one leads you astray.
Many will come in my name, saying, I am, and they will lead many astray.
When you hear of wars and reports of wars, do not be troubled; these things must happen, but the end is not yet.
Nation will rise against nation and reign against reign; there will be earthquakes in various places, and famines. These are the beginning of birth-pains.
But you, watch yourselves. They will hand you over to councils, and you will be beaten in synagogues; you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them.
The good message must first be proclaimed to all nations.
When they lead you away and hand you over, do not worry beforehand what you should speak; but whatever is given to you in that hour, speak that, for it is not you speaking but the holy Spirit.
Brother will hand over brother to death, and father child; children will rise against parents and put them to death.
You will be hated by all because of my name, but the one enduring to the end will be saved.
When you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not, let the reader understand, then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
The one on the roof must not go down or enter to take anything out of the house.
The one in the field must not turn back to take the cloak.
Woe to those carrying in the womb and nursing infants in those days.
Pray that it may not happen in winter.
For those days will be pressure such as has not happened from the beginning of creation that the Divine created until now, and will not happen again.
Unless the Lord shortened the days, no flesh would be saved; but because of the chosen whom he chose, he shortened the days.
Then if anyone says to you, Look, here is the Messiah, or, Look, there, do not trust it.
For false messiahs and false prophets will arise and give signs and wonders, to lead astray, if possible, the chosen.
But you, watch; I have told you all things beforehand.
But in those days, after that pressure, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light.
The stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
Then they will see the Son of Humanity coming in clouds with great power and glory.
Then he will send the messengers and gather his chosen from the four winds, from the end of earth to the end of heaven.
From the fig tree learn the parable: when its branch becomes tender and puts out leaves, you know summer is near.
So also, when you see these things happening, know that it is near, at the doors.
Amen, I say to you: this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things happen.
Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will certainly not pass away.
Concerning that day or hour, no one knows, not the messengers in heaven, nor the Son, but the Father.
Watch; stay awake. For you do not know when the time is.
It is like a person away on a journey, who left his house, gave authority to his servants, to each one the work, and commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.
Therefore keep watch, for you do not know when the lord of the house comes: evening, midnight, rooster-sound, or morning,
lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
What I say to you, I say to all: Keep watch.
